content.problemStatement:
Chef has a tree GGG with NNN nodes (numbered 111 through NNN) and N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 undirected edges. For each node uuu in the tree, he can assign directions to the edges in such a way that uuu is reachable from all the other nodes. Let CuC_uCu‚Äã be the number of valid topological orderings in the resulting directed acyclic graph.

This way, Chef obtains NNN pairs (C1,1),(C2,2),‚Ä¶,(CN,N)(C_1,1), (C_2,2), \ldots, (C_N,N)(C1‚Äã,1),(C2‚Äã,2),‚Ä¶,(CN‚Äã,N) and he sorts all these pairs (Ci,i)(C_i, i)(Ci‚Äã,i) in descending order (first by CiC_iCi‚Äã and in the case of a tie by iii).

For example, consider the tree below:



The sorted list of pairs is ((2,2),(1,3),(1,1))((2,2), (1,3), (1,1))((2,2),(1,3),(1,1)).

Chef quizzes you on this list ‚Äï he wants you to find the KKK-th pair in the sorted list. To make that task easier, Chef only asks for K=1K = 1K=1 (the largest pair) or K=2K = 2K=2 (the second largest pair). Since the number of topological orderings can be very large, compute it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
constraints: the graph described on the input is a tree
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5‚ãÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚ãÖ105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
Each of the next N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv, denoting that nodes uuu and vvv are connected by an edge.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing two space-separated integers uuu and CuC_uCu‚Äã modulo 109+710^9+7109+7, where (Cu,u)(C_u, u)(Cu‚Äã,u) is the KKK-th pair in the sorted list.
